category_title: Confident students with articulation-based Spoken Language Disorders
minimal_context: The student is participating in a peer turn-and-talk activity about who inspires them following an interactive read-aloud of 'Rocket Writes a Story'.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We just finished reading 'Rocket Writes a Story'. Now it is time to turn and talk with your group. Who is someone that inspires you?

STUDENT: I will start the conversation.

TUTOR: Okay, go ahead.

STUDENT: I know my answer.

TUTOR: Tell us.

STUDENT: My sister. She sires [sic] me to get my homework done. My sister sires me [sic].

options.A: Require the student to connect the interactive read-aloud to her own life by completing a written response letter about the text.
options.B: Maintain a ratio of praise to correction at 3:1 or 4:1 during the interactive read-aloud and the subsequent peer turn-and-talk conversations.
options.C: Seat the student at the front of the group during the interactive read-aloud sessions and the subsequent peer turn-and-talk activities.
options.D: Introduce the novel word with a child-friendly explanation, a relatable example, and a description of its use in the text, followed by turn-and-talk opportunities.
